details.useCountryCode: Use country code "PL" instead of "POL" for Poland.

issues.NIP_IN_WRONG_FIELD: Polish **NIP** should be in `NIP` field, not `NrVatUE`
issues.PODMIOT3_UDZIAL_REQUIRES_ROLE_4: Share allowed only for role `4` (property rights holder)
issues.RODZAJ_FAKTURY_SECTIONS: Missing required section for invoice type
issues.FOREIGN_CURRENCY_TAX_PLN: Missing **PLN** tax conversion for foreign currency
issues.ZWOLNIENIE_LOGIC: Exemption logic error: exactly one of `P_19` or `P_19N` required
issues.NST_LOGIC: Transport logic error: exactly one of `P_22` or `P_22N` required
issues.PMARZY_LOGIC: Margin procedure logic error: exactly one of `P_PMarzy` or `P_PMarzyN` required
issues.OO_RATE_FOREIGN_BUYER: Use `np I` or `np II` for foreign buyers, not `oo`
issues.PAYMENT_ZAPLACONO_DATE: Missing payment date when marked as paid
issues.INVALID_BANK_ACCOUNT_FORMAT: Polish bank account (`NrRB`) must be **28** characters in **IBAN** format (`PL` + **26** digits) or **26** digits in NRB format
issues.NEGATIVE_QUANTITY_NOT_ALLOWED: Negative quantities only allowed in corrective invoices
issues.CURRENCY_RATE_MISMATCH: KursWaluty ({actual}) differs from the NBP rate on {date} ({expected})

errors.invalidRootElement: No declaration for {field} in **XSD** schema. Check **XML** namespace
errors.invalidPattern: Value {value} doesn't match required pattern for {field}

expectedValues.maxDecimalPlaces: Max {count, plural, one {# decimal place} other {# decimal places}}

meta.title: Terms of Use — ksefuj.to
meta.description: Terms of use for ksefuj.to — a free, open-source KSeF FA(3) invoice validator.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: ksefuj.to is a free, open-source tool for validating KSeF FA(3) invoices. By using this tool, you accept the following terms.

license.title: 1. Open Source License (Apache 2.0)
license.content: The source code of ksefuj.to is released under the Apache 2.0 license. You may freely use, modify, and distribute it — including commercially — provided you retain copyright and license notices and indicate any changes made. The license is available on GitHub.

noWarranties.title: 2. No Warranties
noWarranties.content: The tool is provided "as-is", without any warranties — express or implied. We do not guarantee that the validator will catch every error, or that validation results will be complete or free of mistakes.

liability.title: 3. Limitation of Liability
liability.content: ksefuj.to is not tax or legal advice. The authors are not responsible for errors in invoices, KSeF submission problems, tax penalties, or any other losses resulting from use of the tool. Final responsibility for invoice correctness rests with the user.

userResponsibility.title: 4. User Responsibility
userResponsibility.content: You are responsible for verifying the correctness of your invoices before submitting them to the KSeF system. The tool helps detect common errors, but does not replace verification by an accountant or your own knowledge of applicable regulations.

freeForever.title: 5. Free Forever
freeForever.content: The validator on ksefuj.to is and will remain free — no limits, no hidden costs, no user accounts, no paywall. We believe essential validation tools should be accessible to everyone working with KSeF.

privacy.title: 6. Privacy
privacy.content: Your XML files never leave your browser. All processing happens locally — no invoice data is sent to any server. See the Privacy Policy for details.

governingLaw.title: 7. Governing Law
governingLaw.content: These terms are governed by Polish law. Any disputes will be resolved by the competent Polish courts.

contact.title: 8. Contact
contact.content: Questions about these terms? Contact us at:

privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy for ksefuj.to — your invoice data never leaves the browser.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Your privacy matters. ksefuj is designed so that your invoice data never leaves your browser. Here's exactly what that means.

noCollection.title: No Data Collection
noCollection.description: ksefuj does not collect, store, or process any personal data. All XML invoice validation happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your files are never uploaded to any server.

noStorage.title: No Server Storage
noStorage.description: Your XML invoice files never leave your device. There is no backend, no database, and no server-side processing. Once you close the browser tab, the data is gone — we never had access to it in the first place.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.description: ksefuj does not use tracking cookies for advertising. The only cookie that may be set is for remembering your language preference. We use Amplitude Analytics which operates without cookies, using anonymized data instead.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Services
thirdParty.description: The site is hosted on Vercel. We use Amplitude Analytics (EU zone — data stays in the European Union) and Vercel Speed Insights. Amplitude captures page views and the interactions we explicitly instrument (e.g. button clicks) to help us understand how the tool is used. Invoice content is never captured. Vercel Speed Insights collects aggregated performance data. This data is used solely for diagnostics and improvement; we do not use it to identify individuals.

noAccounts.title: No User Accounts
noAccounts.description: ksefuj does not require registration, login, or any form of user account. You can use all features without providing any personal information.

openSource.title: Open Source & Auditable
openSource.description: ksefuj is fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license. You can inspect, audit, and verify the entire codebase on GitHub at github.com/ksefuj/ksefuj. What you see is what runs in your browser.

changes.title: Changes to This Policy
changes.description: If we ever change this privacy policy, the update will be reflected on this page with a new date. Since our core principle is that no data leaves your browser, significant changes are unlikely.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: If you have questions about this privacy policy, contact us at hej@ksefuj.to.

faq.question: What is KSeF and who must use it from April 1, 2026?
faq.answer: KSeF (Krajowy System e-Faktur / National e-Invoice System) is Poland's central system by the Ministry of Finance for issuing and receiving structured XML invoices. From February 1, 2026, large companies (2024 turnover above PLN 200 million) must use KSeF. From April 1, 2026, all VAT-registered taxpayers, including sole traders (JDG) and freelancers, are required to use it. Micro-entities (monthly sales up to PLN 10,000) are exempt until end of 2026.

faq.question: What happens if I don't issue an invoice through KSeF?
faq.answer: In 2026, a transitional protection period applies — no financial penalties are imposed for KSeF violations. From January 1, 2027, penalties of up to 100% of the VAT amount on the invoice, or up to 18.7% of the gross invoice value, may be applied. Traditional paper invoices and PDFs will no longer be recognized as valid accounting documents in domestic B2B transactions.

faq.question: What is the FA(3) XML format?
faq.answer: FA(3) is the official XML schema published by Poland's Ministry of Finance that defines the structure of structured invoices in KSeF. Every invoice must comply with this schema — containing required fields in strict order (the XSD uses xs:sequence). The schema covers seller and buyer data, invoice line items, VAT rates, annotations, and many other elements.

faq.question: How do I validate my KSeF invoice before submitting?
faq.answer: Use the free validator at ksefuj.to — drag and drop your XML file, and the tool checks compliance with the XSD schema plus semantic rules that meet official Ministry of Finance requirements. Validation runs entirely in your browser — your data never leaves your computer. You can also use the npm package @ksefuj/validator or the CLI tool.

faq.question: Can I use KSeF without accounting software?
faq.answer: Yes. The Ministry of Finance provides a free Taxpayer Application at ap.ksef.mf.gov.pl that allows you to issue and receive invoices directly in KSeF. Tools like ksefuj.to help validate XML before submission, and will soon enable invoice generation without needing accounting software.

faq.question: What is a KSeF number (KSeF ID)?
faq.answer: A KSeF number is a unique identifier assigned to each invoice when it is accepted by the KSeF system. It confirms that the invoice has been properly registered and is legally binding. This number is required for corrective invoices and when referencing invoices within the system.

faq.question: How do I handle buyers who don't have a NIP?
faq.answer: Invoices for individuals not running a business (B2C) do not require the buyer's NIP. Such invoices are issued normally through KSeF, but the buyer does not have access to the KSeF system — the invoice can be delivered to them by other means (e.g., printout, PDF with QR code).

faq.question: What about foreign buyers and B2C invoices in KSeF?
faq.answer: Invoices for foreign contractors must also go through KSeF. In the FA(3) schema, the NrVatUE field (not NIP) is used for foreign buyers, and VAT rates should be marked as "np" (not applicable) with the appropriate designation (np I or np II). A readable copy of the invoice (e.g., PDF with QR code) can be sent to the foreign buyer additionally.

faq.question: What are the penalties for KSeF non-compliance?
faq.answer: In 2026, a protection period applies with no financial penalties. From January 1, 2027, penalties can reach up to 100% of the VAT amount on the invoice or up to 18.7% of the gross value (when VAT is not separately stated). Penalties apply for not issuing invoices through KSeF, delays in uploading offline-issued invoices, and data errors. The tax authority will assess each case individually.

faq.question: Is there a free KSeF validator?
faq.answer: Yes — ksefuj.to is a completely free, open-source KSeF FA(3) invoice validator. It checks XML compliance with the XSD schema and verifies business rules that meet official Ministry of Finance requirements. It runs entirely in your browser (your data never leaves your computer), and is also available as an npm package (@ksefuj/validator) and CLI tool. No limits, no registration, no fees.
